title: thalictrum gallicum proles timeroyi

constituents: Thalictrum gallicum proles timeroyi has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which possess significant medicinal properties. These constituents include flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and cancer. The roots of Thalictrum gallicum proles timeroyi contain alkaloids and flavonoids, including timorenine and timorenyl, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The flowers and fruits of Thalictrum gallicum proles timeroyi also contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to their potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand their effects.
side_effects: Thalictrum gallicum proles timeroyi can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingestion of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the toxic compounds present in it.
